old_claim.note: Construction provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because Classical Monumental Construction does not represent the iron, winding, commutator, or machine-fabrication capabilities documented for the dynamo.

source_shape.source_locator: Pages 368-369 describe soft-iron field structures, insulated windings, a rotating armature, and a commutator as the apparatus; no building or monumental-construction system is part of the machine.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source identifies electrical-machine components rather than civil or monumental construction capabilities.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge reused a generic construction label whose node scope is unrelated to the documented fabrication boundary of a nineteenth-century electrical machine.

ontology_before: Dynamo inherited from a broad Classical Monumental Construction node through a templated enabling claim.
ontology_after: Self-Excited Industrial Dynamo retains only its generator predecessor and directly evidenced insulated-winding input.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: dynamo no longer directly depends on construction.
would_reject_if: The construction node is respecified to mean electrical-machine fabrication rather than classical monumental works.
A primary source identifies civil construction as part of the scoped 1866 dynamo machine.
The node is broadened to encompass complete generating stations and their buildings.
short_reason: Monumental construction is outside the machine's component boundary.
